data.epi_content:
"Money, where did I put the money?"

Last time, I opened my eyes while drinking tea.

This time, I opened my eyes while pouring hot water over tea leaves.

Thanks to that, I burned my thigh with hot water again, but it's a problem that will be solved quickly.

Searching through my memories, I recalled putting a few gold coins in the drawer. When I checked, there were 7 gold coins inside.

I put the coins in my front pocket, fixed my clothes, put on my shoes, and left the room.

"Ah."

Something must have gotten in my eye; it was itching like crazy.

Tears were streaming down, my nose tingled, and emotions welled up for no reason.

"I want to drink oolong tea.

Coffee would be nice with it too."

In that moment, I impulsively did something crazy.

I could make excuses that the situation drove me mad, but I don't think blowing my head off in front of someone is the right thing to do.

I've seen it so many times, but what does it matter?

For you, death has just become an everyday occurrence.

I suppose so.

Feeling a sudden throb in my head, I got up and started walking.

I thought I'd shed all the etiquette ingrained in me, but with my body intact, I walk with such elegance.

Perhaps my body had become so weak these past few days that I couldn't walk properly, and in my delusion, I thought I'd gotten rid of those mannerisms.

"Ice-filled cheap tea, time-wasting novels, games, movies, and cigarettes I can't even smoke."

I walked to the specialty shop where I bought cigarettes last time and looked for the most expensive tea leaves. I was torn between one that was refreshing and another that was heavy in the mouth but light in aroma.

In the past, I would have just taken both, but I only had enough money for one.

"What are you looking at so intently?"

"I'm trying to choose tea leaves, but I'm not sure which to pick."

The Crown Prince approached me.

He seems to be showing quite an interest in me.

I can guess the reason, but if that's the case, I should keep my distance in case of assassination...

Ah, the shadow. No need to worry about assassination.

Whatever it is, he has people under him who can perform magic that lets them detach and reattach arms.

"Those two are fine, but I also recommend this one for its good aroma."

Saying that, the Crown Prince picked up three containers of tea leaves, paid for them, and handed them to me.

When I looked at him questioningly, he smiled slightly and spoke as if it were nothing.

"I owe your family a small debt.

Take it, and if you ask next time, I'll buy you more.

Consider it the boundless grace of the Crown Prince."

I suppose hatred is a kind of debt too.

To me, he just looks like a terrible person, but what can I do?

Just like I wore a dog headband and collar in front of Lydia and laughed while acting silly, I should bare my stomach and lie down before the Crown Prince, swearing loyalty.

I should say it's fine even if he stabs me in the belly anytime.

Unable to say such things out loud, I simply replied with one word.

"...Thank you."

I appreciated that I didn't have to talk about imperial grace or anything like that.

He wasn't really an authoritarian personality.

I accepted the tea leaves and was about to step out of the store.

But a black hand floating in the air grabbed my shoulder and stopped me.
data.epi_content2:
The Crown Prince asked in a low voice, quiet enough that others couldn't hear.

"Are you going to die? Looking at your face, it seems you already know everything that's happened."

"Well, not right now at least. I might die when I'm an old lady."

"Well, that's fine with me.

If you're going to die, as quietly as possible in your room..."

"You mean you'd prefer I hang myself quietly and die alone, right?"

At my blunt words, the Crown Prince made a somewhat uncomfortable expression and answered.

"...Yes."

"I'll do that this time."

The phrase "this time" seemed to catch his attention, and the Crown Prince's face darkened slightly before he nodded.

I entered the dormitory building while receiving glances from the children around.

Unlike last time, Evan should be wandering around here, but he's nowhere to be seen.

I wonder why, though I'm not particularly curious.

After climbing the stairs for quite a while, I entered my room and took out a teacup.

I put in the tea leaves and poured hot water over them.

It was the tea the Crown Prince had recommended.

It looks like something between green tea and black tea, with a slightly yellowish tint.

As soon as I poured the water, the distinctive aroma of green tea rose up, along with a unique smell as if it had been roasted in a pot and then fermented.

When I tasted it, there wasn't any astringency at all.

"You have such refined taste."

My whole body relaxes.

Soon Evan will come looking for me, and I'll be cooped up in my room again.

This time, I should stock up on lots of food.

Delicious ones.

And then, I'll keep turning back time little by little.

Whenever I get tired and exhausted, I'll put it to my head and let the bullet stir my brain.

Putting the gun to my temple and pulling the trigger was relatively less painful.

And my head remained relatively intact, I think.

What I saw before was someone putting a rifle to their head and shooting, which blew off the entire top part.

Maybe because it's a pistol, it seems to resolve things quite gentlemanly.

"I don't want to die, but do I have to die to not die?"

If I don't slap Vivian's cheek, would something be resolved?

Actually, the downfall began right after I slapped Vivian's cheek, but wasn't it already destined to collapse?

The novel didn't detail the reasons for the family's downfall so specifically.

It was probably just a vague setting that became reality when applied to this world, with things like "the imperial will" and such manifesting in reality.

"I wish Father hadn't died."

I throw the teacup containing the Crown Prince's recommended tea at the picture of my family.

Crash!

Now that's a dramatic sound.

Indeed, when glass breaks, the crashing sound is better than the cracking sound.

When a glass cup just cracks weakly, it feels unpleasant somehow.

"Why, you want to save your brother too."

Then words from my heart start creeping out of my mouth.

"I want to save him."
data.epi_content3:
I take out some cheap black tea from the storage place and put it in a new teacup.

When I pour hot water over it, a strong and bitter black tea brews.

"What should I do? Before brewing tea, I was pouring hot water, so how many minutes was that?

About 5-minute intervals? Then to go back to before the young lady's brother died, and to avoid being called a traitor, how many days back should I go?

In the end, how many times do I have to die to turn back time enough to half-save the family...?"

Not knowing how to make ice, I just swirled lukewarm water around with the wind and then sprinkled it over the strong black tea.

When I drank it, it didn't taste like oolong tea, but it definitely had that cheap tea feeling.

Somehow liking it, I threw all the newly bought tea leaves onto the bed and continued to brew and drink black tea.

Ah, I wonder if my head will be filled with the British love for tea, but nothing seems better for holding onto my thoughts.

No matter how much I turn back time, there's no way to keep the family intact.

It's destined to fall, so all I can do is take care of the people who work there and secure enough assets for them to make a living.

When the world wants it and the Emperor wants it, when God wants it and humans want it, how can I escape it?

"You've gotten used to it, haven't you?"

"I don't know, have you gotten used to it?"

"If only the family were intact, we wouldn't have to live so miserably. Isn't it a business that suits our means?"

"Having to die hundreds or thousands of times... that's the problem. You feel the same horrible pain as I do."

"That's true, let's rest! And when we get tired of resting, we'll escape to something more painful.

And this time, let's drive a nail into people's hearts before we go.

As His Highness Johannes said, sadly, quietly, and lonely in the room."

A nail in the heart.

I certainly seem to have done something quite harsh.

Someone like me dared to cause displeasure to the protagonist Vivian, and Evan, who might be a supporting character or a lead, I'm not sure.

Perhaps I deserve punishment.

"Fine."

I continued this self-dialogue for quite some time.

A girl talking to herself while smiling at the mirror.

I'm suddenly grateful that I live in a world where I have a room to myself.

If I had lived with someone, it would have been terrible for them.

Living with a woman who has nothing good about her, has a nasty personality, and looks like—no, might actually be—a lunatic.

Just imagining it makes me nauseous.

"While drinking tea, let's stop."

Am I cutting you with a knife, or blowing your head off with a pistol?

Right now, I'm just drinking tea.

I'm tired.

I endlessly drank tea, put down the cup, and brewed new contents.

As if this young lady had found the first thing she could do well on her own.

I had learned it, of course, but when would I have had the chance to drink tea and coffee by myself like this so often?

I usually preferred to buy it.

To the point where I couldn't give up the impulse, there were still so many things I had stripped away, set aside, and abandoned.

Knock. Knock. Knock.

The thoughts spinning in my head while killing time fly away with the knocking sound at the door.

It's a bit late, but it's probably Evan who's come.

Not probably, almost certainly.

"Now, I'm tired of looking at each other's faces, getting angry, and being sarcastic."

I look in the mirror to check if there's anything on my face, walk to the entrance, and open the door.

"...Well, if it gets too hard, I can just escape a little faster."
